           Branch data     Line data    Source code
       1                 :            : /*-------------------------------------------------------------------------
       2                 :            :  *
       3                 :            :  * inval.c
       4                 :            :  *        POSTGRES cache invalidation dispatcher code.
       5                 :            :  *
       6                 :            :  *      This is subtle stuff, so pay attention:
       7                 :            :  *
       8                 :            :  *      When a tuple is updated or deleted, our standard time qualification rules
       9                 :            :  *      consider that it is *still valid* so long as we are in the same command,
      10                 :            :  *      ie, until the next CommandCounterIncrement() or transaction commit.
      11                 :            :  *      (See utils/time/tqual.c, and note that system catalogs are generally
      12                 :            :  *      scanned under SnapshotNow rules by the system, or plain user snapshots
      13                 :            :  *      for user queries.)      At the command boundary, the old tuple stops
      14                 :            :  *      being valid and the new version, if any, becomes valid.  Therefore,
      15                 :            :  *      we cannot simply flush a tuple from the system caches during heap_update()
      16                 :            :  *      or heap_delete().  The tuple is still good at that point; what's more,
      17                 :            :  *      even if we did flush it, it might be reloaded into the caches by a later
      18                 :            :  *      request in the same command.  So the correct behavior is to keep a list
      19                 :            :  *      of outdated (updated/deleted) tuples and then do the required cache
      20                 :            :  *      flushes at the next command boundary.  We must also keep track of
      21                 :            :  *      inserted tuples so that we can flush "negative" cache entries that match
      22                 :            :  *      the new tuples; again, that mustn't happen until end of command.
      23                 :            :  *
      24                 :            :  *      Once we have finished the command, we still need to remember inserted
      25                 :            :  *      tuples (including new versions of updated tuples), so that we can flush
      26                 :            :  *      them from the caches if we abort the transaction.  Similarly, we'd better
      27                 :            :  *      be able to flush "negative" cache entries that may have been loaded in
      28                 :            :  *      place of deleted tuples, so we still need the deleted ones too.
      29                 :            :  *
      30                 :            :  *      If we successfully complete the transaction, we have to broadcast all
      31                 :            :  *      these invalidation events to other backends (via the SI message queue)
      32                 :            :  *      so that they can flush obsolete entries from their caches.      Note we have
      33                 :            :  *      to record the transaction commit before sending SI messages, otherwise
      34                 :            :  *      the other backends won't see our updated tuples as good.
      35                 :            :  *
      36                 :            :  *      When a subtransaction aborts, we can process and discard any events
      37                 :            :  *      it has queued.  When a subtransaction commits, we just add its events
      38                 :            :  *      to the pending lists of the parent transaction.
      39                 :            :  *
      40                 :            :  *      In short, we need to remember until xact end every insert or delete
      41                 :            :  *      of a tuple that might be in the system caches.  Updates are treated as
      42                 :            :  *      two events, delete + insert, for simplicity.  (There are cases where
      43                 :            :  *      it'd be possible to record just one event, but we don't currently try.)
      44                 :            :  *
      45                 :            :  *      We do not need to register EVERY tuple operation in this way, just those
      46                 :            :  *      on tuples in relations that have associated catcaches.  We do, however,
      47                 :            :  *      have to register every operation on every tuple that *could* be in a
      48                 :            :  *      catcache, whether or not it currently is in our cache.  Also, if the
      49                 :            :  *      tuple is in a relation that has multiple catcaches, we need to register
      50                 :            :  *      an invalidation message for each such catcache.  catcache.c's
      51                 :            :  *      PrepareToInvalidateCacheTuple() routine provides the knowledge of which
      52                 :            :  *      catcaches may need invalidation for a given tuple.
      53                 :            :  *
      54                 :            :  *      Also, whenever we see an operation on a pg_class or pg_attribute tuple,
      55                 :            :  *      we register a relcache flush operation for the relation described by that
      56                 :            :  *      tuple.  pg_class updates trigger an smgr flush operation as well.
      57                 :            :  *
      58                 :            :  *      We keep the relcache and smgr flush requests in lists separate from the
      59                 :            :  *      catcache tuple flush requests.  This allows us to issue all the pending
      60                 :            :  *      catcache flushes before we issue relcache flushes, which saves us from
      61                 :            :  *      loading a catcache tuple during relcache load only to flush it again
      62                 :            :  *      right away.  Also, we avoid queuing multiple relcache flush requests for
      63                 :            :  *      the same relation, since a relcache flush is relatively expensive to do.
      64                 :            :  *      (XXX is it worth testing likewise for duplicate catcache flush entries?
      65                 :            :  *      Probably not.)
      66                 :            :  *
      67                 :            :  *      If a relcache flush is issued for a system relation that we preload
      68                 :            :  *      from the relcache init file, we must also delete the init file so that
      69                 :            :  *      it will be rebuilt during the next backend restart.  The actual work of
      70                 :            :  *      manipulating the init file is in relcache.c, but we keep track of the
      71                 :            :  *      need for it here.
      72                 :            :  *
      73                 :            :  *      The request lists proper are kept in CurTransactionContext of their
      74                 :            :  *      creating (sub)transaction, since they can be forgotten on abort of that
      75                 :            :  *      transaction but must be kept till top-level commit otherwise.  For
      76                 :            :  *      simplicity we keep the controlling list-of-lists in TopTransactionContext.
      77                 :            :  *
      78                 :            :  *
      79                 :            :  * Portions Copyright (c) 1996-2008, PostgreSQL Global Development Group
      80                 :            :  * Portions Copyright (c) 1994, Regents of the University of California
      81                 :            :  *
      82                 :            :  * IDENTIFICATION
      83                 :            :  *        $PostgreSQL: pgsql/src/backend/utils/cache/inval.c,v 1.83.2.1 2008/03/13 18:00:39 tgl Exp $
      84                 :            :  *
      85                 :            :  *-------------------------------------------------------------------------
      86                 :            :  */
      87                 :            : #include "postgres.h"
      88                 :            : 
      89                 :            : #include "access/twophase_rmgr.h"
      90                 :            : #include "access/xact.h"
      91                 :            : #include "catalog/catalog.h"
      92                 :            : #include "catalog/gp_policy.h"
      93                 :            : #include "cdb/cdbvars.h"
      94                 :            : #include "miscadmin.h"
      95                 :            : #include "storage/sinval.h"
      96                 :            : #include "storage/smgr.h"
      97                 :            : #include "utils/inval.h"
      98                 :            : #include "utils/memutils.h"
      99                 :            : #include "utils/relcache.h"
     100                 :            : #include "utils/simex.h"
     101                 :            : #include "utils/syscache.h"
     102                 :            : 
     103                 :            : 
     104                 :            : /*
     105                 :            :  * To minimize palloc traffic, we keep pending requests in successively-
     106                 :            :  * larger chunks (a slightly more sophisticated version of an expansible
     107                 :            :  * array).      All request types can be stored as SharedInvalidationMessage
     108                 :            :  * records.  The ordering of requests within a list is never significant.
     109                 :            :  */
     110                 :            : typedef struct InvalidationChunk
     111                 :            : {
     112                 :            :         struct InvalidationChunk *next;         /* list link */
     113                 :            :         int                     nitems;                 /* # items currently stored in chunk */
     114                 :            :         int                     maxitems;               /* size of allocated array in this chunk */
     115                 :            :         SharedInvalidationMessage msgs[1];      /* VARIABLE LENGTH ARRAY */
     116                 :            : } InvalidationChunk;                    /* VARIABLE LENGTH STRUCTURE */
     117                 :            : 
     118                 :            : typedef struct InvalidationListHeader
     119                 :            : {
     120                 :            :         InvalidationChunk *cclist;      /* list of chunks holding catcache msgs */
     121                 :            :         InvalidationChunk *rclist;      /* list of chunks holding relcache/smgr msgs */
     122                 :            : } InvalidationListHeader;
     123                 :            : 
     124                 :            : /*----------------
     125                 :            :  * Invalidation info is divided into two lists:
     126                 :            :  *      1) events so far in current command, not yet reflected to caches.
     127                 :            :  *      2) events in previous commands of current transaction; these have
     128                 :            :  *         been reflected to local caches, and must be either broadcast to
     129                 :            :  *         other backends or rolled back from local cache when we commit
     130                 :            :  *         or abort the transaction.
     131                 :            :  * Actually, we need two such lists for each level of nested transaction,
     132                 :            :  * so that we can discard events from an aborted subtransaction.  When
     133                 :            :  * a subtransaction commits, we append its lists to the parent's lists.
     134                 :            :  *
     135                 :            :  * The relcache-file-invalidated flag can just be a simple boolean,
     136                 :            :  * since we only act on it at transaction commit; we don't care which
     137                 :            :  * command of the transaction set it.
     138                 :            :  *----------------
     139                 :            :  */
     140                 :            : 
     141                 :            : typedef struct TransInvalidationInfo
     142                 :            : {
     143                 :            :         /* Back link to parent transaction's info */
     144                 :            :         struct TransInvalidationInfo *parent;
     145                 :            : 
     146                 :            :         /* Subtransaction nesting depth */
     147                 :            :         int                     my_level;
     148                 :            : 
     149                 :            :         /* head of current-command event list */
     150                 :            :         InvalidationListHeader CurrentCmdInvalidMsgs;
     151                 :            : 
     152                 :            :         /* head of previous-commands event list */
     153                 :            :         InvalidationListHeader PriorCmdInvalidMsgs;
     154                 :            : 
     155                 :            :         /* init file must be invalidated? */
     156                 :            :         bool            RelcacheInitFileInval;
     157                 :            : } TransInvalidationInfo;
     158                 :            : 
     159                 :            : static TransInvalidationInfo *transInvalInfo = NULL;
     160                 :            : 
     161                 :            : /*
     162                 :            :  * Dynamically-registered callback functions.  Current implementation
     163                 :            :  * assumes there won't be very many of these at once; could improve if needed.
     164                 :            :  */
     165                 :            : /*
     166                 :            :  * MAX_SYSCACHE_CALLBACKS has been bumped up in GPDB, because ORCA registers
     167                 :            :  * a lot of callbacks.
     168                 :            :  */
     169                 :            : #define MAX_SYSCACHE_CALLBACKS 40
     170                 :            : #define MAX_RELCACHE_CALLBACKS 5
     171                 :            : 
     172                 :            : static struct SYSCACHECALLBACK
     173                 :            : {
     174                 :            :         int16           id;                             /* cache number */
     175                 :            :         SyscacheCallbackFunction function;
     176                 :            :         Datum           arg;
     177                 :            : }       syscache_callback_list[MAX_SYSCACHE_CALLBACKS];
     178                 :            : 
     179                 :            : static int      syscache_callback_count = 0;
     180                 :            : 
     181                 :            : static struct RELCACHECALLBACK
     182                 :            : {
     183                 :            :         RelcacheCallbackFunction function;
     184                 :            :         Datum           arg;
     185                 :            : }       relcache_callback_list[MAX_RELCACHE_CALLBACKS];
     186                 :            : 
     187                 :            : static int      relcache_callback_count = 0;
     188                 :            : 
     189                 :            : /* info values for 2PC callback */
     190                 :            : #define TWOPHASE_INFO_MSG                       0       /* SharedInvalidationMessage */
     191                 :            : #define TWOPHASE_INFO_FILE_BEFORE       1       /* relcache file inval */
     192                 :            : #define TWOPHASE_INFO_FILE_AFTER        2       /* relcache file inval */
     193                 :            : 
     194                 :            : static void PersistInvalidationMessage(SharedInvalidationMessage *msg);
     195                 :            : 
     196                 :            : 
     197                 :            : /* ----------------------------------------------------------------
     198                 :            :  *                              Invalidation list support functions
     199                 :            :  *
     200                 :            :  * These three routines encapsulate processing of the "chunked"
     201                 :            :  * representation of what is logically just a list of messages.
     202                 :            :  * ----------------------------------------------------------------
     203                 :            :  */
     204                 :            : 
     205                 :            : /*
     206                 :            :  * AddInvalidationMessage
     207                 :            :  *              Add an invalidation message to a list (of chunks).
     208                 :            :  *
     209                 :            :  * Note that we do not pay any great attention to maintaining the original
     210                 :            :  * ordering of the messages.
     211                 :            :  */
     212                 :            : static void
     213                 :            : AddInvalidationMessage(InvalidationChunk **listHdr,
     214                 :            :                                            SharedInvalidationMessage *msg)
     215                 :            : {
     216                 :          0 :         InvalidationChunk *chunk = *listHdr;
     217                 :            : 
     218         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (chunk == NULL)
     219                 :            :         {
     220                 :            :                 /* First time through; create initial chunk */
     221                 :            : #define FIRSTCHUNKSIZE 16
     222                 :          0 :                 chunk = (InvalidationChunk *)
     223                 :          0 :                         MemoryContextAlloc(CurTransactionContext,
     224                 :            :                                                            sizeof(InvalidationChunk) +
     225                 :            :                                         (FIRSTCHUNKSIZE - 1) *sizeof(SharedInvalidationMessage));
     226                 :          0 :                 chunk->nitems = 0;
     227                 :          0 :                 chunk->maxitems = FIRSTCHUNKSIZE;
     228                 :          0 :                 chunk->next = *listHdr;
     229                 :          0 :                 *listHdr = chunk;
     230                 :          0 :         }
     231         [ #  # ]:          0 :         else if (chunk->nitems >= chunk->maxitems)
     232                 :            :         {
     233                 :            :                 /* Need another chunk; double size of last chunk */
     234                 :          0 :                 int                     chunksize = 2 * chunk->maxitems;
     235                 :            : 
     236                 :          0 :                 chunk = (InvalidationChunk *)
     237                 :          0 :                         MemoryContextAlloc(CurTransactionContext,
     238                 :            :                                                            sizeof(InvalidationChunk) +
     239                 :            :                                                  (chunksize - 1) *sizeof(SharedInvalidationMessage));
     240                 :          0 :                 chunk->nitems = 0;
     241                 :          0 :                 chunk->maxitems = chunksize;
     242                 :          0 :                 chunk->next = *listHdr;
     243                 :          0 :                 *listHdr = chunk;
     244                 :          0 :         }
     245                 :            :         /* Okay, add message to current chunk */
     246                 :          0 :         chunk->msgs[chunk->nitems] = *msg;
     247                 :          0 :         chunk->nitems++;
     248                 :          0 : }
     249                 :            : 
     250                 :            : /*
     251                 :            :  * Append one list of invalidation message chunks to another, resetting
     252                 :            :  * the source chunk-list pointer to NULL.
     253                 :            :  */
     254                 :            : static void
     255                 :            : AppendInvalidationMessageList(InvalidationChunk **destHdr,
     256                 :            :                                                           InvalidationChunk **srcHdr)
     257                 :            : {
     258                 :          0 :         InvalidationChunk *chunk = *srcHdr;
     259                 :            : 
     260         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (chunk == NULL)
     261                 :          0 :                 return;                                 /* nothing to do */
     262                 :            : 
     263         [ #  # ]:          0 :         while (chunk->next != NULL)
     264                 :          0 :                 chunk = chunk->next;
     265                 :            : 
     266                 :          0 :         chunk->next = *destHdr;
     267                 :            : 
     268                 :          0 :         *destHdr = *srcHdr;
     269                 :            : 
     270                 :          0 :         *srcHdr = NULL;
     271                 :          0 : }
     272                 :            : 
     273                 :            : /*
     274                 :            :  * Process a list of invalidation messages.
     275                 :            :  *
     276                 :            :  * This is a macro that executes the given code fragment for each message in
     277                 :            :  * a message chunk list.  The fragment should refer to the message as *msg.
     278                 :            :  */
     279                 :            : #define ProcessMessageList(listHdr, codeFragment) \
     280                 :            :         do { \
     281                 :            :                 InvalidationChunk *_chunk; \
     282                 :            :                 for (_chunk = (listHdr); _chunk != NULL; _chunk = _chunk->next) \
     283                 :            :                 { \
     284                 :            :                         int             _cindex; \
     285                 :            :                         for (_cindex = 0; _cindex < _chunk->nitems; _cindex++) \
     286                 :            :                         { \
     287                 :            :                                 SharedInvalidationMessage *msg = &_chunk->msgs[_cindex]; \
     288                 :            :                                 codeFragment; \
     289                 :            :                         } \
     290                 :            :                 } \
     291                 :            :         } while (0)
     292                 :            : 
     293                 :            : /*
     294                 :            :  * Process a list of invalidation messages group-wise.
     295                 :            :  *
     296                 :            :  * As above, but the code fragment can handle an array of messages.
     297                 :            :  * The fragment should refer to the messages as msgs[], with n entries.
     298                 :            :  */
     299                 :            : #define ProcessMessageListMulti(listHdr, codeFragment) \
     300                 :            :         do { \
     301                 :            :                 InvalidationChunk *_chunk; \
     302                 :            :                 for (_chunk = (listHdr); _chunk != NULL; _chunk = _chunk->next) \
     303                 :            :                 { \
     304                 :            :                         SharedInvalidationMessage *msgs = _chunk->msgs; \
     305                 :            :                         int             n = _chunk->nitems; \
     306                 :            :                         codeFragment; \
     307                 :            :                 } \
     308                 :            :         } while (0)
     309                 :            : 
     310                 :            : 
     311                 :            : /* ----------------------------------------------------------------
     312                 :            :  *                              Invalidation set support functions
     313                 :            :  *
     314                 :            :  * These routines understand about the division of a logical invalidation
     315                 :            :  * list into separate physical lists for catcache and relcache/smgr entries.
     316                 :            :  * ----------------------------------------------------------------
     317                 :            :  */
     318                 :            : 
     319                 :            : /*
     320                 :            :  * Add a catcache inval entry
     321                 :            :  */
     322                 :            : static void
     323                 :            : AddCatcacheInvalidationMessage(InvalidationListHeader *hdr,
     324                 :            :                                                            int id, uint32 hashValue,
     325                 :            :                                                            ItemPointer tuplePtr, Oid dbId)
     326                 :            : {
     327                 :            :         SharedInvalidationMessage msg;
     328                 :            : 
     329                 :          0 :         msg.cc.id = (int16) id;
     330                 :          0 :         msg.cc.tuplePtr = *tuplePtr;
     331                 :          0 :         msg.cc.dbId = dbId;
     332                 :          0 :         msg.cc.hashValue = hashValue;
     333                 :          0 :         AddInvalidationMessage(&hdr->cclist, &msg);
     334                 :          0 : }
     335                 :            : 
     336                 :            : /*
     337                 :            :  * Add a relcache inval entry
     338                 :            :  */
     339                 :            : static void
     340                 :            : AddRelcacheInvalidationMessage(InvalidationListHeader *hdr,
     341                 :            :                                                            Oid dbId, Oid relId)
     342                 :            : {
     343                 :            :         SharedInvalidationMessage msg;
     344                 :            : 
     345                 :            :         /* Don't add a duplicate item */
     346                 :            :         /* We assume dbId need not be checked because it will never change */
     347 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         ProcessMessageList(hdr->rclist,
         [ #  # ][ #  # ]
     348                 :            :                                            if (msg->rc.id == SHAREDINVALRELCACHE_ID &&
     349                 :            :                                                    msg->rc.relId == relId)
     350                 :            :                                            return);
     351                 :            : 
     352                 :            :         /* OK, add the item */
     353                 :          0 :         msg.rc.id = SHAREDINVALRELCACHE_ID;
     354                 :          0 :         msg.rc.dbId = dbId;
     355                 :          0 :         msg.rc.relId = relId;
     356                 :          0 :         AddInvalidationMessage(&hdr->rclist, &msg);
     357                 :          0 : }
     358                 :            : 
     359                 :            : /*
     360                 :            :  * Add an smgr inval entry
     361                 :            :  */
     362                 :            : static void
     363                 :            : AddSmgrInvalidationMessage(InvalidationListHeader *hdr,
     364                 :            :                                                    RelFileNode rnode)
     365                 :            : {
     366                 :            :         SharedInvalidationMessage msg;
     367                 :            : 
     368                 :            :         /* Don't add a duplicate item */
     369 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         ProcessMessageList(hdr->rclist,
         [ #  # ][ #  # ]
         [ #  # ][ #  # ]
     370                 :            :                                            if (msg->sm.id == SHAREDINVALSMGR_ID &&
     371                 :            :                                                    RelFileNodeEquals(msg->sm.rnode, rnode))
     372                 :            :                                            return);
     373                 :            : 
     374                 :            :         /* OK, add the item */
     375                 :          0 :         msg.sm.id = SHAREDINVALSMGR_ID;
     376                 :          0 :         msg.sm.rnode = rnode;
     377                 :          0 :         AddInvalidationMessage(&hdr->rclist, &msg);
     378                 :          0 : }
     379                 :            : 
     380                 :            : /*
     381                 :            :  * Append one list of invalidation messages to another, resetting
     382                 :            :  * the source list to empty.
     383                 :            :  */
     384                 :            : static void
     385                 :            : AppendInvalidationMessages(InvalidationListHeader *dest,
     386                 :            :                                                    InvalidationListHeader *src)
     387                 :            : {
     388                 :          0 :         AppendInvalidationMessageList(&dest->cclist, &src->cclist);
     389                 :          0 :         AppendInvalidationMessageList(&dest->rclist, &src->rclist);
     390                 :          0 : }
     391                 :            : 
     392                 :            : /*
     393                 :            :  * Execute the given function for all the messages in an invalidation list.
     394                 :            :  * The list is not altered.
     395                 :            :  *
     396                 :            :  * catcache entries are processed first, for reasons mentioned above.
     397                 :            :  */
     398                 :            : static void
     399                 :            : ProcessInvalidationMessages(InvalidationListHeader *hdr,
     400                 :            :                                                         void (*func) (SharedInvalidationMessage *msg))
     401                 :            : {
     402 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         ProcessMessageList(hdr->cclist, func(msg));
     403 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         ProcessMessageList(hdr->rclist, func(msg));
     404                 :          0 : }
     405                 :            : 
     406                 :            : /*
     407                 :            :  * As above, but the function is able to process an array of messages
     408                 :            :  * rather than just one at a time.
     409                 :            :  */
     410                 :            : static void
     411                 :            : ProcessInvalidationMessageMulti(InvalidationListHeader *hdr,
     412                 :            :                                  void (*func) (const SharedInvalidationMessage *msgs, int n))
     413                 :            : {
     414         [ #  # ]:          0 :         ProcessMessageListMulti(hdr->cclist, func(msgs, n));
     415         [ #  # ]:          0 :         ProcessMessageListMulti(hdr->rclist, func(msgs, n));
     416                 :          0 : }
     417                 :            : 
     418                 :            : /* ----------------------------------------------------------------
     419                 :            :  *                                        private support functions
     420                 :            :  * ----------------------------------------------------------------
     421                 :            :  */
     422                 :            : 
     423                 :            : /*
     424                 :            :  * RegisterCatcacheInvalidation
     425                 :            :  *
     426                 :            :  * Register an invalidation event for a catcache tuple entry.
     427                 :            :  */
     428                 :            : static void
     429                 :            : RegisterCatcacheInvalidation(int cacheId,
     430                 :            :                                                          uint32 hashValue,
     431                 :            :                                                          ItemPointer tuplePtr,
     432                 :            :                                                          Oid dbId)
     433                 :            : {
     434                 :          0 :         AddCatcacheInvalidationMessage(&transInvalInfo->CurrentCmdInvalidMsgs,
     435                 :          0 :                                                                    cacheId, hashValue, tuplePtr, dbId);
     436                 :          0 : }
     437                 :            : 
     438                 :            : /*
     439                 :            :  * RegisterRelcacheInvalidation
     440                 :            :  *
     441                 :            :  * As above, but register a relcache invalidation event.
     442                 :            :  */
     443                 :            : static void
     444                 :            : RegisterRelcacheInvalidation(Oid dbId, Oid relId)
     445                 :            : {
     446                 :          0 :         AddRelcacheInvalidationMessage(&transInvalInfo->CurrentCmdInvalidMsgs,
     447                 :          0 :                                                                    dbId, relId);
     448                 :            : 
     449                 :            :         /*
     450                 :            :          * Most of the time, relcache invalidation is associated with system
     451                 :            :          * catalog updates, but there are a few cases where it isn't.  Quick
     452                 :            :          * hack to ensure that the next CommandCounterIncrement() will think
     453                 :            :          * that we need to do CommandEndInvalidationMessages().
     454                 :            :          */
     455                 :          0 :         (void) GetCurrentCommandId(true);
     456                 :            : 
     457                 :            :         /*
     458                 :            :          * If the relation being invalidated is one of those cached in the
     459                 :            :          * relcache init file, mark that we need to zap that file at commit.
     460                 :            :          */
     461         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (RelationIdIsInInitFile(relId))
     462                 :          0 :                 transInvalInfo->RelcacheInitFileInval = true;
     463                 :          0 : }
     464                 :            : 
     465                 :            : /*
     466                 :            :  * RegisterSmgrInvalidation
     467                 :            :  *
     468                 :            :  * As above, but register an smgr invalidation event.
     469                 :            :  */
     470                 :            : static void
     471                 :            : RegisterSmgrInvalidation(RelFileNode rnode)
     472                 :            : {
     473                 :          0 :         AddSmgrInvalidationMessage(&transInvalInfo->CurrentCmdInvalidMsgs,
     474                 :            :                                                            rnode);
     475                 :            : 
     476                 :            :         /*
     477                 :            :          * As above, just in case there is not an associated catalog change.
     478                 :            :          */
     479                 :          0 :         (void) GetCurrentCommandId(true);
     480                 :          0 : }
     481                 :            : 
     482                 :            : #ifdef USE_ASSERT_CHECKING
     483                 :            : static char *
     484                 :            : si_to_str(SharedInvalidationMessage *msg)
     485                 :            : {
     486                 :            :         StringInfoData buf;
     487                 :            :         int i;
     488                 :            :         char *s;
     489                 :            : 
     490                 :          0 :         initStringInfo(&buf);
     491                 :          0 :         appendStringInfo(&buf, "message id = %d", msg->id);
     492                 :          0 :         s = (char *)&(msg->cc);
     493         [ #  # ]:          0 :         for (i = 0; i < sizeof(SharedInvalCatcacheMsg); i++)
     494                 :            :         {
     495         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (i == 0)
     496                 :          0 :                         appendStringInfo(&buf, " CC:");
     497                 :            : 
     498                 :          0 :                 appendStringInfo(&buf, " %x", *(s + i));
     499                 :            : 
     500                 :          0 :         }
     501                 :          0 :         s = (char *)&(msg->rc);
     502         [ #  # ]:          0 :         for (i = 0; i < sizeof(SharedInvalRelcacheMsg); i++)
     503                 :            :         {
     504         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (i == 0)
     505                 :          0 :                         appendStringInfo(&buf, " RC:");
     506                 :            : 
     507                 :          0 :                 appendStringInfo(&buf, " %x", *(s + i));
     508                 :            : 
     509                 :          0 :         }
     510                 :          0 :         return buf.data;
     511                 :            : }
     512                 :            : #endif
     513                 :            : 
     514                 :            : /*
     515                 :            :  * LocalExecuteInvalidationMessage
     516                 :            :  *
     517                 :            :  * Process a single invalidation message (which could be of any type).
     518                 :            :  * Only the local caches are flushed; this does not transmit the message
     519                 :            :  * to other backends.
     520                 :            :  */
     521                 :            : static void
     522                 :            : LocalExecuteInvalidationMessage(SharedInvalidationMessage *msg)
     523                 :            : {
     524                 :            :         int                     i;
     525                 :            : 
     526         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (msg->id >= 0)
     527                 :            :         {
     528 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (msg->cc.dbId == MyDatabaseId || msg->cc.dbId == 0)
     529                 :            :                 {
     530                 :          0 :                         CatalogCacheIdInvalidate(msg->cc.id,
     531                 :          0 :                                                                          msg->cc.hashValue,
     532                 :          0 :                                                                          &msg->cc.tuplePtr);
     533                 :            : 
     534         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         for (i = 0; i < syscache_callback_count; i++)
     535                 :            :                         {
     536                 :          0 :                                 struct SYSCACHECALLBACK *ccitem = syscache_callback_list + i;
     537                 :            : 
     538         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 if (ccitem->id == msg->cc.id)
     539                 :          0 :                                         (*ccitem->function) (ccitem->arg,
     540                 :          0 :                                                                                  msg->cc.id, &msg->cc.tuplePtr);
     541                 :          0 :                         }
     542                 :          0 :                 }
     543                 :          0 :         }
     544         [ #  # ]:          0 :         else if (msg->id == SHAREDINVALRELCACHE_ID)
     545                 :            :         {
     546 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (msg->rc.dbId == MyDatabaseId || msg->rc.dbId == InvalidOid)
     547                 :            :                 {
     548                 :          0 :                         RelationCacheInvalidateEntry(msg->rc.relId);
     549                 :            : 
     550         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         for (i = 0; i < relcache_callback_count; i++)
     551                 :            :                         {
     552                 :          0 :                                 struct RELCACHECALLBACK *ccitem = relcache_callback_list + i;
     553                 :            : 
     554                 :          0 :                                 (*ccitem->function) (ccitem->arg, msg->rc.relId);
     555                 :          0 :                         }
     556                 :          0 :                 }
     557                 :          0 :         }
     558         [ #  # ]:          0 :         else if (msg->id == SHAREDINVALSMGR_ID)
     559                 :            :         {
     560                 :            :                 /*
     561                 :            :                  * We could have smgr entries for relations of other databases, so no
     562                 :            :                  * short-circuit test is possible here.
     563                 :            :                  */
     564                 :          0 :                 smgrclosenode(msg->sm.rnode);
     565                 :          0 :         }
     566                 :            :         else
     567                 :            :         {
     568                 :            : #ifdef USE_ASSERT_CHECKING
     569                 :          0 :                 elog(NOTICE, "invalid SI message: %s", si_to_str(msg));
     570                 :            : #endif
     571                 :          0 :                 elog(FATAL, "unrecognized SI message id: %d", msg->id);
     572                 :            :         }
     573                 :          0 : }
     574                 :            : 
     575                 :            : /*
     576                 :            :  *              InvalidateSystemCaches
     577                 :            :  *
     578                 :            :  *              This blows away all tuples in the system catalog caches and
     579                 :            :  *              all the cached relation descriptors and smgr cache entries.
     580                 :            :  *              Relation descriptors that have positive refcounts are then rebuilt.
     581                 :            :  *
     582                 :            :  *              We call this when we see a shared-inval-queue overflow signal,
     583                 :            :  *              since that tells us we've lost some shared-inval messages and hence
     584                 :            :  *              don't know what needs to be invalidated.
     585                 :            :  */
     586                 :            : static void
     587                 :            : InvalidateSystemCaches(void)
     588                 :            : {
     589                 :            :         int                     i;
     590                 :            : 
     591                 :          0 :         ResetCatalogCaches();
     592                 :          0 :         RelationCacheInvalidate();      /* gets smgr cache too */
     593                 :            : 
     594         [ #  # ]:          0 :         for (i = 0; i < syscache_callback_count; i++)
     595                 :            :         {
     596                 :          0 :                 struct SYSCACHECALLBACK *ccitem = syscache_callback_list + i;
     597                 :            : 
     598                 :          0 :                 (*ccitem->function) (ccitem->arg, ccitem->id, NULL);
     599                 :          0 :         }
     600                 :            : 
     601         [ #  # ]:          0 :         for (i = 0; i < relcache_callback_count; i++)
     602                 :            :         {
     603                 :          0 :                 struct RELCACHECALLBACK *ccitem = relcache_callback_list + i;
     604                 :            : 
     605                 :          0 :                 (*ccitem->function) (ccitem->arg, InvalidOid);
     606                 :          0 :         }
     607                 :          0 : }
     608                 :            : 
     609                 :            : /*
     610                 :            :  * PrepareForTupleInvalidation
     611                 :            :  *              Detect whether invalidation of this tuple implies invalidation
     612                 :            :  *              of catalog/relation cache entries; if so, register inval events.
     613                 :            :  */
     614                 :            : static void
     615                 :            : PrepareForTupleInvalidation(Relation relation, HeapTuple tuple)
     616                 :            : {
     617                 :            :         Oid                     tupleRelId;
     618                 :            :         Oid                     databaseId;
     619                 :            :         Oid                     relationId;
     620                 :            : 
     621                 :            :         /* Do nothing during bootstrap */
     622         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (IsBootstrapProcessingMode())
     623                 :          0 :                 return;
     624                 :            : 
     625                 :            :         /*
     626                 :            :          * We only need to worry about invalidation for tuples that are in system
     627                 :            :          * relations; user-relation tuples are never in catcaches and can't affect
     628                 :            :          * the relcache either.
     629                 :            :          */
     630         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (!IsSystemRelation(relation))
     631                 :          0 :                 return;
     632                 :            : 
     633                 :            :         /*
     634                 :            :          * TOAST tuples can likewise be ignored here. Note that TOAST tables are
     635                 :            :          * considered system relations so they are not filtered by the above test.
     636                 :            :          */
     637         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (IsToastRelation(relation))
     638                 :          0 :                 return;
     639                 :            : 
     640                 :            :         /*
     641                 :            :          * First let the catcache do its thing
     642                 :            :          */
     643                 :          0 :         PrepareToInvalidateCacheTuple(relation, tuple,
     644                 :            :                                                                   RegisterCatcacheInvalidation);
     645                 :            : 
     646                 :            :         /*
     647                 :            :          * Now, is this tuple one of the primary definers of a relcache entry?
     648                 :            :          */
     649                 :          0 :         tupleRelId = RelationGetRelid(relation);
     650                 :            : 
     651         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (tupleRelId == RelationRelationId)
     652                 :            :         {
     653                 :          0 :                 Form_pg_class classtup = (Form_pg_class) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
     654                 :            :                 RelFileNode rnode;
     655                 :            : 
     656         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 relationId = HeapTupleGetOid(tuple);
     657         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (classtup->relisshared)
     658                 :          0 :                         databaseId = InvalidOid;
     659                 :            :                 else
     660                 :          0 :                         databaseId = MyDatabaseId;
     661                 :            : 
     662                 :            :                 /*
     663                 :            :                  * We need to send out an smgr inval as well as a relcache inval. This
     664                 :            :                  * is needed because other backends might possibly possess smgr cache
     665                 :            :                  * but not relcache entries for the target relation.
     666                 :            :                  *
     667                 :            :                  * Note: during a pg_class row update that assigns a new relfilenode
     668                 :            :                  * or reltablespace value, we will be called on both the old and new
     669                 :            :                  * tuples, and thus will broadcast invalidation messages showing both
     670                 :            :                  * the old and new RelFileNode values.  This ensures that other
     671                 :            :                  * backends will close smgr references to the old file.
     672                 :            :                  *
     673                 :            :                  * XXX possible future cleanup: it might be better to trigger smgr
     674                 :            :                  * flushes explicitly, rather than indirectly from pg_class updates.
     675                 :            :                  */
     676         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (classtup->reltablespace)
     677                 :          0 :                         rnode.spcNode = classtup->reltablespace;
     678                 :            :                 else
     679                 :          0 :                         rnode.spcNode = MyDatabaseTableSpace;
     680                 :          0 :                 rnode.dbNode = databaseId;
     681                 :          0 :                 rnode.relNode = classtup->relfilenode;
     682                 :          0 :                 RegisterSmgrInvalidation(rnode);
     683                 :          0 :         }
     684         [ #  # ]:          0 :         else if (tupleRelId == AttributeRelationId)
     685                 :            :         {
     686                 :          0 :                 Form_pg_attribute atttup = (Form_pg_attribute) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
     687                 :            : 
     688                 :          0 :                 relationId = atttup->attrelid;
     689                 :            : 
     690                 :            :                 /*
     691                 :            :                  * KLUGE ALERT: we always send the relcache event with MyDatabaseId,
     692                 :            :                  * even if the rel in question is shared (which we can't easily tell).
     693                 :            :                  * This essentially means that only backends in this same database
     694                 :            :                  * will react to the relcache flush request.  This is in fact
     695                 :            :                  * appropriate, since only those backends could see our pg_attribute
     696                 :            :                  * change anyway.  It looks a bit ugly though.  (In practice, shared
     697                 :            :                  * relations can't have schema changes after bootstrap, so we should
     698                 :            :                  * never come here for a shared rel anyway.)
     699                 :            :                  */
     700                 :          0 :                 databaseId = MyDatabaseId;
     701                 :          0 :         }
     702         [ #  # ]:          0 :         else if (tupleRelId == GpPolicyRelationId)
     703                 :            :         {
     704                 :          0 :                 FormData_gp_policy *gptup = (FormData_gp_policy *) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
     705                 :            : 
     706                 :          0 :                 relationId = gptup->localoid;
     707                 :          0 :                 databaseId = MyDatabaseId;
     708                 :          0 :         }
     709         [ #  # ]:          0 :         else if (tupleRelId == IndexRelationId)
     710                 :            :         {
     711                 :          0 :                 Form_pg_index indextup = (Form_pg_index) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
     712                 :            : 
     713                 :            :                 /*
     714                 :            :                  * When a pg_index row is updated, we should send out a relcache inval
     715                 :            :                  * for the index relation.      As above, we don't know the shared status
     716                 :            :                  * of the index, but in practice it doesn't matter since indexes of
     717                 :            :                  * shared catalogs can't have such updates.
     718                 :            :                  */
     719                 :          0 :                 relationId = indextup->indexrelid;
     720                 :          0 :                 databaseId = MyDatabaseId;
     721                 :          0 :         }
     722                 :            :         else
     723                 :          0 :                 return;
     724                 :            : 
     725                 :            :         /*
     726                 :            :          * Yes.  We need to register a relcache invalidation event.
     727                 :            :          */
     728                 :          0 :         RegisterRelcacheInvalidation(databaseId, relationId);
     729                 :          0 : }
     730                 :            : 
     731                 :            : 
     732                 :            : /* ----------------------------------------------------------------
     733                 :            :  *                                        public functions
     734                 :            :  * ----------------------------------------------------------------
     735                 :            :  */
     736                 :            : 
     737                 :            : /*
     738                 :            :  * AcceptInvalidationMessages
     739                 :            :  *              Read and process invalidation messages from the shared invalidation
     740                 :            :  *              message queue.
     741                 :            :  *
     742                 :            :  * Note:
     743                 :            :  *              This should be called as the first step in processing a transaction.
     744                 :            :  */
     745                 :            : void
     746                 :            : AcceptInvalidationMessages(void)
     747                 :            : {
     748                 :          0 :         ReceiveSharedInvalidMessages(LocalExecuteInvalidationMessage,
     749                 :            :                                                                  InvalidateSystemCaches);
     750                 :            : 
     751 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         Assert(SysCacheFlushForce_IsValid(gp_test_system_cache_flush_force));
                 [ #  # ]
     752                 :            : 
     753                 :            : #ifdef USE_TEST_UTILS
     754                 :            :         /*
     755                 :            :          * Test code to force cache flushes anytime a flush could happen.
     756                 :            :          *
     757                 :            :          * If used with CLOBBER_FREED_MEMORY, gp_test_system_cache_flush_force provides
     758                 :            :          * a fairly thorough test that the system contains no cache-flush hazards.
     759                 :            :          * However, it also makes the system unbelievably slow --- the regression
     760                 :            :          * tests take about 100 times longer than normal.
     761                 :            :          *
     762                 :            :          * gp_test_system_cache_flush_force_recursive slows things by
     763                 :            :          * at least a factor of 10000, so I wouldn't suggest
     764                 :            :          * trying to run the entire regression tests that way.  It's useful to try
     765                 :            :          * a few simple tests, to make sure that cache reload isn't subject to
     766                 :            :          * internal cache-flush hazards, but after you've done a few thousand
     767                 :            :          * recursive reloads it's unlikely you'll learn more.
     768                 :            :          */
     769                 :            :         if (SysCacheFlushForce_Recursive == gp_test_system_cache_flush_force)
     770                 :            :         {
     771                 :            :                 /* potentially recursive cache invalidation */
     772                 :            :                 InvalidateSystemCaches();
     773                 :            :         }
     774                 :            :         else
     775                 :            :         {
     776                 :            :                 static bool in_recursion = false;
     777                 :            : 
     778                 :            :                 if (!in_recursion)
     779                 :            :                 {
     780                 :            :                         bool invalidate = (SysCacheFlushForce_NonRecursive == gp_test_system_cache_flush_force);
     781                 :            : 
     782                 :            :                         if (!invalidate &&
     783                 :            :                                 gp_simex_init &&
     784                 :            :                                 gp_simex_run && 
     785                 :            :                                 gp_simex_class == SimExESClass_CacheInvalidation)
     786                 :            :                         {
     787                 :            :                                 /*
     788                 :            :                                  * Same basic idea as above, except using the SimEx facility, the main
     789                 :            :                                  * advantage of this approach is that it only triggers the invalidation
     790                 :            :                                  * once per unique call stack, which should make testing significantly
     791                 :            :                                  * faster.
     792                 :            :                                  */
     793                 :            :                                 invalidate = (SimExESSubClass_CacheInvalidation == SimEx_CheckInject());
     794                 :            :                         }
     795                 :            : 
     796                 :            :                         if (invalidate)
     797                 :            :                         {
     798                 :            :                                 /* avoid recursive cache invalidation */
     799                 :            :                                 in_recursion = true;
     800                 :            :                                 InvalidateSystemCaches();
     801                 :            :                                 in_recursion = false;
     802                 :            :                         }
     803                 :            :                 }
     804                 :            :         }
     805                 :            : #endif
     806                 :          0 : }
     807                 :            : 
     808                 :            : /*
     809                 :            :  * AtStart_Inval
     810                 :            :  *              Initialize inval lists at start of a main transaction.
     811                 :            :  */
     812                 :            : void
     813                 :            : AtStart_Inval(void)
     814                 :            : {
     815 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         Assert(transInvalInfo == NULL);
     816                 :          0 :         transInvalInfo = (TransInvalidationInfo *)
     817                 :          0 :                 MemoryContextAllocZero(TopTransactionContext,
     818                 :            :                                                            sizeof(TransInvalidationInfo));
     819                 :          0 :         transInvalInfo->my_level = GetCurrentTransactionNestLevel();
     820                 :          0 : }
     821                 :            : 
     822                 :            : /*
     823                 :            :  * AtPrepare_Inval
     824                 :            :  *              Save the inval lists state at 2PC transaction prepare.
     825                 :            :  *
     826                 :            :  * In this phase we just generate 2PC records for all the pending invalidation
     827                 :            :  * work.
     828                 :            :  */
     829                 :            : void
     830                 :            : AtPrepare_Inval(void)
     831                 :            : {
     832                 :            :         /* Must be at top of stack */
     833 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         Assert(transInvalInfo != NULL && transInvalInfo->parent == NULL);
                 [ #  # ]
     834                 :            : 
     835                 :            :         /*
     836                 :            :          * Relcache init file invalidation requires processing both before and
     837                 :            :          * after we send the SI messages.
     838                 :            :          */
     839         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (transInvalInfo->RelcacheInitFileInval)
     840                 :          0 :                 RegisterTwoPhaseRecord(TWOPHASE_RM_INVAL_ID, TWOPHASE_INFO_FILE_BEFORE,
     841                 :            :                                                            NULL, 0);
     842                 :            : 
     843                 :          0 :         AppendInvalidationMessages(&transInvalInfo->PriorCmdInvalidMsgs,
     844                 :          0 :                                                            &transInvalInfo->CurrentCmdInvalidMsgs);
     845                 :            : 
     846                 :          0 :         ProcessInvalidationMessages(&transInvalInfo->PriorCmdInvalidMsgs,
     847                 :            :                                                                 PersistInvalidationMessage);
     848                 :            : 
     849         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (transInvalInfo->RelcacheInitFileInval)
     850                 :          0 :                 RegisterTwoPhaseRecord(TWOPHASE_RM_INVAL_ID, TWOPHASE_INFO_FILE_AFTER,
     851                 :            :                                                            NULL, 0);
     852                 :          0 : }
     853                 :            : 
     854                 :            : /*
     855                 :            :  * PostPrepare_Inval
     856                 :            :  *              Clean up after successful PREPARE.
     857                 :            :  *
     858                 :            :  * Here, we want to act as though the transaction aborted, so that we will
     859                 :            :  * undo any syscache changes it made, thereby bringing us into sync with the
     860                 :            :  * outside world, which doesn't believe the transaction committed yet.
     861                 :            :  *
     862                 :            :  * If the prepared transaction is later aborted, there is nothing more to
     863                 :            :  * do; if it commits, we will receive the consequent inval messages just
     864                 :            :  * like everyone else.
     865                 :            :  */
     866                 :            : void
     867                 :            : PostPrepare_Inval(void)
     868                 :            : {
     869                 :          0 :         AtEOXact_Inval(false);
     870                 :          0 : }
     871                 :            : 
     872                 :            : /*
     873                 :            :  * AtSubStart_Inval
     874                 :            :  *              Initialize inval lists at start of a subtransaction.
     875                 :            :  */
     876                 :            : void
     877                 :            : AtSubStart_Inval(void)
     878                 :            : {
     879                 :            :         TransInvalidationInfo *myInfo;
     880                 :            : 
     881 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         Assert(transInvalInfo != NULL);
     882                 :          0 :         myInfo = (TransInvalidationInfo *)
     883                 :          0 :                 MemoryContextAllocZero(TopTransactionContext,
     884                 :            :                                                            sizeof(TransInvalidationInfo));
     885                 :          0 :         myInfo->parent = transInvalInfo;
     886                 :          0 :         myInfo->my_level = GetCurrentTransactionNestLevel();
     887                 :          0 :         transInvalInfo = myInfo;
     888                 :          0 : }
     889                 :            : 
     890                 :            : /*
     891                 :            :  * PersistInvalidationMessage
     892                 :            :  *              Write an invalidation message to the 2PC state file.
     893                 :            :  */
     894                 :            : static void
     895                 :            : PersistInvalidationMessage(SharedInvalidationMessage *msg)
     896                 :            : {
     897                 :          0 :         RegisterTwoPhaseRecord(TWOPHASE_RM_INVAL_ID, TWOPHASE_INFO_MSG,
     898                 :          0 :                                                    msg, sizeof(SharedInvalidationMessage));
     899                 :          0 : }
     900                 :            : 
     901                 :            : /*
     902                 :            :  * inval_twophase_postcommit
     903                 :            :  *              Process an invalidation message from the 2PC state file.
     904                 :            :  */
     905                 :            : void
     906                 :            : inval_twophase_postcommit(TransactionId xid, uint16 info,
     907                 :            :                                                   void *recdata, uint32 len)
     908                 :            : {
     909                 :            :         SharedInvalidationMessage *msg;
     910                 :            : 
     911   [ #  #  #  # ]:          0 :         switch (info)
     912                 :            :         {
     913                 :            :                 case TWOPHASE_INFO_MSG:
     914                 :          0 :                         msg = (SharedInvalidationMessage *) recdata;
     915 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                         Assert(len == sizeof(SharedInvalidationMessage));
     916                 :          0 :                         SendSharedInvalidMessages(msg, 1);
     917                 :          0 :                         break;
     918                 :            :                 case TWOPHASE_INFO_FILE_BEFORE:
     919                 :          0 :                         RelationCacheInitFilePreInvalidate();
     920                 :          0 :                         break;
     921                 :            :                 case TWOPHASE_INFO_FILE_AFTER:
     922                 :          0 :                         RelationCacheInitFilePostInvalidate();
     923                 :          0 :                         break;
     924                 :            :                 default:
     925         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         Assert(false);
     926                 :          0 :                         break;
     927                 :            :         }
     928                 :          0 : }
     929                 :            : 
     930                 :            : 
     931                 :            : /*
     932                 :            :  * AtEOXact_Inval
     933                 :            :  *              Process queued-up invalidation messages at end of main transaction.
     934                 :            :  *
     935                 :            :  * If isCommit, we must send out the messages in our PriorCmdInvalidMsgs list
     936                 :            :  * to the shared invalidation message queue.  Note that these will be read
     937                 :            :  * not only by other backends, but also by our own backend at the next
     938                 :            :  * transaction start (via AcceptInvalidationMessages).  This means that
     939                 :            :  * we can skip immediate local processing of anything that's still in
     940                 :            :  * CurrentCmdInvalidMsgs, and just send that list out too.
     941                 :            :  *
     942                 :            :  * If not isCommit, we are aborting, and must locally process the messages
     943                 :            :  * in PriorCmdInvalidMsgs.      No messages need be sent to other backends,
     944                 :            :  * since they'll not have seen our changed tuples anyway.  We can forget
     945                 :            :  * about CurrentCmdInvalidMsgs too, since those changes haven't touched
     946                 :            :  * the caches yet.
     947                 :            :  *
     948                 :            :  * In any case, reset the various lists to empty.  We need not physically
     949                 :            :  * free memory here, since TopTransactionContext is about to be emptied
     950                 :            :  * anyway.
     951                 :            :  *
     952                 :            :  * Note:
     953                 :            :  *              This should be called as the last step in processing a transaction.
     954                 :            :  */
     955                 :            : void
     956                 :            : AtEOXact_Inval(bool isCommit)
     957                 :            : {
     958         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (isCommit)
     959                 :            :         {
     960                 :            :                 /* Must be at top of stack */
     961 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 Assert(transInvalInfo != NULL && transInvalInfo->parent == NULL);
                 [ #  # ]
     962                 :            : 
     963                 :            :                 /*
     964                 :            :                  * Relcache init file invalidation requires processing both before and
     965                 :            :                  * after we send the SI messages.  However, we need not do anything
     966                 :            :                  * unless we committed.
     967                 :            :                  */
     968         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (transInvalInfo->RelcacheInitFileInval)
     969                 :          0 :                         RelationCacheInitFilePreInvalidate();
     970                 :            : 
     971                 :          0 :                 AppendInvalidationMessages(&transInvalInfo->PriorCmdInvalidMsgs,
     972                 :          0 :                                                                    &transInvalInfo->CurrentCmdInvalidMsgs);
     973                 :            : 
     974                 :          0 :                 ProcessInvalidationMessageMulti(&transInvalInfo->PriorCmdInvalidMsgs,
     975                 :            :                                                                                 SendSharedInvalidMessages);
     976                 :            : 
     977         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (transInvalInfo->RelcacheInitFileInval)
     978                 :          0 :                         RelationCacheInitFilePostInvalidate();
     979                 :          0 :         }
     980         [ #  # ]:          0 :         else if (transInvalInfo != NULL)
     981                 :            :         {
     982                 :            :                 /* Must be at top of stack */
     983 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 Assert(transInvalInfo->parent == NULL);
     984                 :            : 
     985                 :          0 :                 ProcessInvalidationMessages(&transInvalInfo->PriorCmdInvalidMsgs,
     986                 :            :                                                                         LocalExecuteInvalidationMessage);
     987                 :          0 :         }
     988                 :            : 
     989                 :            :         /* Need not free anything explicitly */
     990                 :          0 :         transInvalInfo = NULL;
     991                 :          0 : }
     992                 :            : 
     993                 :            : /*
     994                 :            :  * AtEOSubXact_Inval
     995                 :            :  *              Process queued-up invalidation messages at end of subtransaction.
     996                 :            :  *
     997                 :            :  * If isCommit, process CurrentCmdInvalidMsgs if any (there probably aren't),
     998                 :            :  * and then attach both CurrentCmdInvalidMsgs and PriorCmdInvalidMsgs to the
     999                 :            :  * parent's PriorCmdInvalidMsgs list.
    1000                 :            :  *
    1001                 :            :  * If not isCommit, we are aborting, and must locally process the messages
    1002                 :            :  * in PriorCmdInvalidMsgs.      No messages need be sent to other backends.
    1003                 :            :  * We can forget about CurrentCmdInvalidMsgs too, since those changes haven't
    1004                 :            :  * touched the caches yet.
    1005                 :            :  *
    1006                 :            :  * In any case, pop the transaction stack.      We need not physically free memory
    1007                 :            :  * here, since CurTransactionContext is about to be emptied anyway
    1008                 :            :  * (if aborting).  Beware of the possibility of aborting the same nesting
    1009                 :            :  * level twice, though.
    1010                 :            :  */
    1011                 :            : void
    1012                 :            : AtEOSubXact_Inval(bool isCommit)
    1013                 :            : {
    1014                 :          0 :         int                     my_level = GetCurrentTransactionNestLevel();
    1015                 :          0 :         TransInvalidationInfo *myInfo = transInvalInfo;
