    1016                 :            : 
    1017         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (isCommit)
    1018                 :            :         {
    1019                 :            :                 /* Must be at non-top of stack */
    1020 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 Assert(myInfo != NULL && myInfo->parent != NULL);
                 [ #  # ]
    1021 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 Assert(myInfo->my_level == my_level);
    1022                 :            : 
    1023                 :            :                 /* If CurrentCmdInvalidMsgs still has anything, fix it */
    1024                 :          0 :                 CommandEndInvalidationMessages();
    1025                 :            : 
    1026                 :            :                 /* Pass up my inval messages to parent */
    1027                 :          0 :                 AppendInvalidationMessages(&myInfo->parent->PriorCmdInvalidMsgs,
    1028                 :          0 :                                                                    &myInfo->PriorCmdInvalidMsgs);
    1029                 :            : 
    1030                 :            :                 /* Pending relcache inval becomes parent's problem too */
    1031         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (myInfo->RelcacheInitFileInval)
    1032                 :          0 :                         myInfo->parent->RelcacheInitFileInval = true;
    1033                 :            : 
    1034                 :            :                 /* Pop the transaction state stack */
    1035                 :          0 :                 transInvalInfo = myInfo->parent;
    1036                 :            : 
    1037                 :            :                 /* Need not free anything else explicitly */
    1038                 :          0 :                 pfree(myInfo);
    1039                 :          0 :         }
    1040 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         else if (myInfo != NULL && myInfo->my_level == my_level)
    1041                 :            :         {
    1042                 :            :                 /* Must be at non-top of stack */
    1043 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 Assert(myInfo->parent != NULL);
    1044                 :            : 
    1045                 :          0 :                 ProcessInvalidationMessages(&myInfo->PriorCmdInvalidMsgs,
    1046                 :            :                                                                         LocalExecuteInvalidationMessage);
    1047                 :            : 
    1048                 :            :                 /* Pop the transaction state stack */
    1049                 :          0 :                 transInvalInfo = myInfo->parent;
    1050                 :            : 
    1051                 :            :                 /* Need not free anything else explicitly */
    1052                 :          0 :                 pfree(myInfo);
    1053                 :          0 :         }
    1054                 :          0 : }

    1056                 :            : /*
    1057                 :            :  * CommandEndInvalidationMessages
    1058                 :            :  *              Process queued-up invalidation messages at end of one command
    1059                 :            :  *              in a transaction.
    1060                 :            :  *
    1061                 :            :  * Here, we send no messages to the shared queue, since we don't know yet if
    1062                 :            :  * we will commit.      We do need to locally process the CurrentCmdInvalidMsgs
    1063                 :            :  * list, so as to flush our caches of any entries we have outdated in the
    1064                 :            :  * current command.  We then move the current-cmd list over to become part
    1065                 :            :  * of the prior-cmds list.

    1067                 :            :  * Note:
    1068                 :            :  *              This should be called during CommandCounterIncrement(),
    1069                 :            :  *              after we have advanced the command ID.

    1071                 :            : void
    1072                 :            : CommandEndInvalidationMessages(void)
    1073                 :            : {
    1074                 :            :         /*
    1075                 :            :          * You might think this shouldn't be called outside any transaction, but
    1076                 :            :          * bootstrap does it, and also ABORT issued when not in a transaction. So
    1077                 :            :          * just quietly return if no state to work on.
    1078                 :            :          */
    1079         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (transInvalInfo == NULL)
    1080                 :          0 :                 return;
    1081                 :            : 
    1082                 :          0 :         ProcessInvalidationMessages(&transInvalInfo->CurrentCmdInvalidMsgs,
    1083                 :            :                                                                 LocalExecuteInvalidationMessage);
    1084                 :          0 :         AppendInvalidationMessages(&transInvalInfo->PriorCmdInvalidMsgs,
    1085                 :          0 :                                                            &transInvalInfo->CurrentCmdInvalidMsgs);
    1086                 :          0 : }

    1088                 :            : 
    1089                 :            : /*
    1090                 :            :  * BeginNonTransactionalInvalidation
    1091                 :            :  *              Prepare for invalidation messages for nontransactional updates.
    1092                 :            :  *
    1093                 :            :  * A nontransactional invalidation is one that must be sent whether or not
    1094                 :            :  * the current transaction eventually commits.  We arrange for all invals
    1095                 :            :  * queued between this call and EndNonTransactionalInvalidation() to be sent
    1096                 :            :  * immediately when the latter is called.
    1097                 :            :  *
    1098                 :            :  * Currently, this is only used by heap_page_prune(), and only when it is
    1099                 :            :  * invoked during VACUUM FULL's first pass over a table.  We expect therefore
    1100                 :            :  * that we are not inside a subtransaction and there are no already-pending
    1101                 :            :  * invalidations.  This could be relaxed by setting up a new nesting level of
    1102                 :            :  * invalidation data, but for now there's no need.  Note that heap_page_prune
    1103                 :            :  * knows that this function does not change any state, and therefore there's
    1104                 :            :  * no need to worry about cleaning up if there's an elog(ERROR) before
    1105                 :            :  * reaching EndNonTransactionalInvalidation (the invals will just be thrown
    1106                 :            :  * away if that happens).
    1107                 :            :  *
    1108                 :            :  * GPDB: In GPDB, however, the way we use transactions during VACUUM FULL is
    1109                 :            :  * different from PostgreSQL, and can already be pending invalidations in
    1110                 :            :  * the queue. So we do what the above suggested, and create a new nesting level.
    1111                 :            :  */
    1112                 :            : void
    1113                 :            : BeginNonTransactionalInvalidation(void)
    1114                 :            : {
    1115                 :            :         TransInvalidationInfo *myInfo;
    1116                 :            : 
    1117                 :            :         /* Must be at top of stack */
    1118 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         Assert(transInvalInfo != NULL && transInvalInfo->parent == NULL);
                 [ #  # ]
    1119                 :            : 
    1120                 :            :         /* GPDB: create a new nesting level, as in AtSubStart_Inval() */
    1121                 :          0 :         myInfo = (TransInvalidationInfo *)
    1122                 :          0 :                 MemoryContextAllocZero(TopTransactionContext,
    1123                 :            :                                                            sizeof(TransInvalidationInfo));
    1124                 :          0 :         myInfo->parent = transInvalInfo;
    1125                 :          0 :         myInfo->my_level = GetCurrentTransactionNestLevel();
    1126                 :          0 :         transInvalInfo = myInfo;
    1127                 :          0 : }
    1128                 :            : 
    1129                 :            : /*
    1130                 :            :  * EndNonTransactionalInvalidation
    1131                 :            :  *              Process queued-up invalidation messages for nontransactional updates.
    1132                 :            :  *
    1133                 :            :  * We expect to find messages in CurrentCmdInvalidMsgs only (else there
    1134                 :            :  * was a CommandCounterIncrement within the "nontransactional" update).
    1135                 :            :  * We must process them locally and send them out to the shared invalidation
    1136                 :            :  * message queue.
    1137                 :            :  *
    1138                 :            :  * We must also reset the lists to empty and explicitly free memory (we can't
    1139                 :            :  * rely on end-of-transaction cleanup for that).
    1140                 :            :  */
    1141                 :            : void
    1142                 :            : EndNonTransactionalInvalidation(void)
    1143                 :            : {
    1144                 :            :         InvalidationChunk *chunk;
    1145                 :            :         InvalidationChunk *next;
    1146                 :            : 
    1147                 :            :         /* Must be at one nesting level below top of stack */
    1148 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         Assert(transInvalInfo != NULL);
    1149 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         Assert(transInvalInfo->parent != NULL);
    1150 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         Assert(transInvalInfo->parent->parent == NULL);
    1151                 :            : 
    1152                 :            :         /* Must not have any prior-command messages */
    1153 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         Assert(transInvalInfo->PriorCmdInvalidMsgs.cclist == NULL);
    1154 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         Assert(transInvalInfo->PriorCmdInvalidMsgs.rclist == NULL);
    1155                 :            : 
    1156                 :            :         /*
    1157                 :            :          * At present, this function is only used for CTID-changing updates;
    1158                 :            :          * since the relcache init file doesn't store any tuple CTIDs, we
    1159                 :            :          * don't have to invalidate it.  That might not be true forever
    1160                 :            :          * though, in which case we'd need code similar to AtEOXact_Inval.
    1161                 :            :          */
    1162                 :            : 
    1163                 :            :         /* Send out the invals */
    1164                 :          0 :         ProcessInvalidationMessages(&transInvalInfo->CurrentCmdInvalidMsgs,
    1165                 :            :                                                                 LocalExecuteInvalidationMessage);
    1166                 :          0 :         ProcessInvalidationMessageMulti(&transInvalInfo->CurrentCmdInvalidMsgs,
    1167                 :            :                                                                         SendSharedInvalidMessages);
    1168                 :            : 
    1169                 :            :         /* Clean up and release memory */

    1171                 :          0 :                  chunk != NULL;
    1172                 :          0 :                  chunk = next)
    1173                 :            :         {
    1174                 :          0 :                 next = chunk->next;
    1175                 :          0 :                 pfree(chunk);
    1176                 :          0 :         }
    1177         [ #  # ]:          0 :         for (chunk = transInvalInfo->CurrentCmdInvalidMsgs.rclist;
    1178                 :          0 :                  chunk != NULL;
    1179                 :          0 :                  chunk = next)
    1180                 :            :         {
    1181                 :          0 :                 next = chunk->next;
    1182                 :          0 :                 pfree(chunk);
    1183                 :          0 :         }
    1184                 :            : 
    1185                 :            :         /* Pop the transaction state stack */
    1186                 :            :         {
    1187                 :          0 :                 TransInvalidationInfo *myInfo = transInvalInfo;
    1188                 :            : 
    1189                 :          0 :                 transInvalInfo = myInfo->parent;
    1190                 :            : 
    1191                 :          0 :                 pfree(myInfo);

    1284                 :            : /*

    1286                 :            :  *              Register the specified function to be called for all future
